metadata.title:
PROHIBTTIONZSTS EXPECT TO


SHOW UP 5000, 900 BALLOTS

pages.raw_text:
There will be at the convention
four delegatesat-large from each
state ami one delegate for every
200 votes cast for DR.. Silas G.
Swallow, the candidate in 1904.
The vote for Swallow was 25S,536;l
so there will be about 1900 votes
in the convention.


Recently the prohibition leaders
predicted that 1,000,00 votes would
be cast for their candidates next
fall Now. they say. the number
may go to 100000, unless the con-
ventlons of the old parties take
action favorable to prohibition.
They base their estimate ON the
work of the national issue move
ment. This is an enterprise of the
prohibtionists to get voters to sign
pledge that they support this year
only such candidates for president
and vice president as will place
themselves on record as favoring
the entire prohibition of the manu-
fact1re and sale of alcoholic liquora
for beverage purposes.


The pledge has already been
signed by over 1000 900 men. 1t is
non partisan, but the prohibitions
lists expect to get all the voters un-
less the old parties nominate men
favorable to prohibition.


Hoke Smith, of Georgia, may b0
nominated for president.
